pattern: Splitting the stones into two piles to minimize their difference is a subset-sum / 0-1 knapsack: find the achievable subset sum closest to half the total.
hints: Each smash assigns a plus or minus sign to a stone, so the final remainder is a signed sum of all weights; you want its smallest non-negative value.
Partition the stones into two groups and minimize the gap between their sums; the answer is total minus twice the best reachable group sum that does not exceed half.
Run a boolean knapsack over reachable subset sums up to total/2, take the largest reachable sum s, and return total minus 2*s.

edge_inputs.description: two equal stones cancel to zero
two unequal stones leave their difference
greedy largest-pair smashing is not optimal here

stress.description: max length with full value range to stress the knapsack table
stress.note: small bounds keep the DP table tiny and fast
